finding.summary: High silence fraction (0.78) dilutes all loudness measurements; the bass is present but very quiet on average, peaking at only -18 dBFS.
finding.probable_cause: The track's volume fader is at 0 dB but the source material contains extended silences (78% dead air), and the non‑silent portions are at a relatively low level.
finding.confidence_reason: The high silence fraction makes it hard to gauge the true loudness of the bass content; the measured integrated LUFS (-20) and RMS (-42) are pulled down by silence, so the actual bass notes might be louder than they appear.

evidence_refs.description: Fraction of the capture that is silence (0.78)
RMS = -42 dB (very low, partly due to silence)
Sample peak = -18 dB (moderate level for the non‑silent parts)
Strong 100 Hz band (-18 dB), weaker highs - typical synth bass

proposal.reason: Increasing the track volume by 6 dB will raise the level of the bass notes, making the track more present and competitive in a mix, while leaving the silence fraction unchanged.
